title: FTR-REVIEW-001-FORMULAS-CORE-MOVE-P3 — Move `rendering/formulas.py` to `core/` and close hexagonal guard gap L1
purpose: rendering/formulas.py contains purely backend-neutral value objects (ListLiteralFormulaSpec, LookupFormulaSpec, and their factories). Because it lives in the rendering/ layer, domain/transformations/enrich_lookup.py and domain/transformations/fk_helpers.py must cross the hexagonal boundary to import it — the only real layer violation in the codebase. Moving the file to core/formulas.py eliminates the violation and allows a guard to enforce the domain ⊥ rendering boundary going forward.

purpose: Nine production modules exceed 440 lines (peaks: 725, 631, 567, 562, 562); one adapter module (io_backends/ods/odf_renderer.py, ~540 lines) is also above the threshold. At the same time, domain/transformations/__init__.py, domain/__init__.py, and

title: FTR-REVIEW-001-MODULE-HEADERS-P5 — Add Purpose/Layer/Imports header to every `*.py`
purpose: Most modules in domain/, core/, rendering/, and pipeline/ have no module-level docstring. head -5 on any of these files gives no orientation for a developer or agent. A lightweight header convention — 1–3 lines stating Purpose, Layer, and allowed project-internal imports — costs little to write and makes every module self-describing without reading its body. This is the highest-leverage token-efficiency improvement for agent-assisted development: head -5 replaces a full-file read for orientation.

title: FTR-REVIEW-001-MULTIHEADER-READBACK-P5 -- Close JSON and CSV MultiIndex readback gaps
purpose: Close the remaining MultiIndex readback asymmetries that REVIEW-001 kept as nice-to-have backend gaps after the original multiheader work.

title: FTR-REVIEW-001-PASSES-CLEANUP-P3 — Split `passes/core.py`, delete stubs, single `default_passes()` source
purpose: rendering/passes/core.py (518 lines, 8 pass classes) is the only real implementation of IR passes, but the passes/ package contains per-pass stub files implying a module-per-pass layout. formula_pass.py and post_pass.py are empty;

title: FTR-REVIEW-001-QUICK-WINS-P3 — Bundle of self-contained quick fixes from REVIEW-001
purpose: Six self-contained fixes from the review are each under 30 minutes and have no dependencies on other clusters. Bundling them in a single PR minimises context-switch overhead while delivering immediate correctness and hygiene value. Each item is independently verifiable. One item (A-002) touches a gated CLI flag and requires explicit developer approval before execution.

title: FTR-SOURCE-STYLE-GUARDRAILS-P4 -- Add source-style guardrails for maintainable AI-assisted code
purpose: Prevent recurring source-level readability drift in AI-assisted development by combining lightweight mechanical checks with clearer agent instructions.

title: FTR-SPARSE-CROSSTABLE-COLLAPSE-P4A — Bijective sparse collapse/expand for crosstables
priority: High (pre-beta, driven by prototype consumer requirements)
purpose: Provide a bijective transform pair (collapse + expand) that suppresses a dominant default value in crosstable cells, yielding a sparse representation that is easier to read and edit in large spreadsheets. The inverse restores the full dense matrix.

title: FTR-SPLIT-BY-DISCRIMINATOR-P4A — Bijective split/merge transform by discriminator column
priority: High (pre-beta, driven by prototype consumer requirements)
purpose: Provide a pair of bijective transforms (split + merge) that partition a single table into multiple sheets by a discriminator column, and reconstitute the original table from those sheets. The discriminator value is encoded in the sheet name; the discriminator column itself is dropped from the split sheets.
